params.text:
Four types of communication

We communicate in more ways than words alone. Tap each card below to reveal what each type means in care.

params.alt: Four types of communication: verbal, non-verbal, written and visual

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Reveal what each type of communication means in care.

dialogs.answer: The words we speak — including tone, pace and volume. Speak clearly, avoid jargon, and check understanding.
Body language, facial expression, eye contact, gestures and touch. Often says more than words — around half of a message is non-verbal.
dialogs.imageAltText: Figure representing body language and non-verbal communication

dialogs.answer: Care plans, daily notes, handovers, texts and emails. Must be clear, factual, timely and legible — others rely on it.
Pictures, symbols, objects of reference, Makaton, hearing aids and communication boards that help a person express themselves.
dialogs.imageAltText: Symbols and picture representing visual and assistive communication
